reasoning: The 1-year real interest rate typically behaves similarly to a random walk over short horizons of 1 to 3 months, making the baseline probability of an increase around 50%. Given that monetary policy in 2024-2025 is broadly expected to be in an easing cycle, there may be slight downward pressure on short-term real rates. Therefore, my estimate for the 1-month horizon (Nov 2025) is slightly below 50% at 49%. For the 3-month horizon (Jan 2026), increasing uncertainty brings the probability back to 50%.
